etymology.description: 亼 (assemble) + 兄 (elder) written twice -> originally depicting people responding together, stylized later as 亼 + 吅 (declare) + 从 (follow) -> consensus, unanimity
etymology.interpretation: The character 僉(qiān, 'all') visually represents the idea of multiple individuals (represented by the repetition of 兄) coming together to answer or declare something as a group. This evolves into a stylization showing an assembly and group consensus, reinforcing the meaning of unanimity or all.
